summary: Trading terminal reliability fixes, desktop share-card save support, and Windows onefile packaging compatibility updates.
release_notes: Entry orders can now submit native bracket TP/SL children in one request flow, with TP/SL levels drawn immediately and the entry line deferred until the real fill price is known.

Improved order reconciliation so orders cancelled directly in TWS stop lingering as Submitted in the app, and single-order cancel actions no longer double-report success while cancellation is still pending.
Close, half-close, and flatten flows now wait for IB to acknowledge TP or other active-order cancellation before sending the exit order, preventing accidental short-sale rejections.
Orders Today no longer falsely shows No orders during account bootstrap, and the trading popup now resolves account context before its first orders and balance refresh.
Level 2 depth handling now filters transient crossed books more aggressively and preserves the last valid book when IB briefly emits an empty depth snapshot, reducing flicker.
Desktop share cards can now be saved through the native pywebview save dialog instead of relying on browser-only download behavior.
Packaged build stamping remains syntax-validated before compile, and Windows onefile builds now pass Nuitka an absolute runtime temp directory instead of an incompatible variable token.
